title: Study Abroad Application
transcript: Student: Hello, I'd like to apply for the study abroad program in Barcelona next semester, but I have some questions first. Advisor: Of course. The Barcelona program is very popular. What would you like to know? Student: I saw that the deadline is March 15th, but I haven't chosen my courses yet. Do I need a complete course plan to apply? Advisor: No, you only need a preliminary plan. You can finalize your courses after acceptance. However, you do need a letter of recommendation and a statement of purpose. Student: I have a professor who agreed to write a recommendation. How should she submit it? Advisor: She can upload it directly through the online portal. Make sure she uses her official university email. Student: Okay. Also, will my financial aid transfer to the Barcelona program? Advisor: Most federal and institutional aid does transfer, but you should confirm with the Financial Aid Office. There may also be additional travel grants available through the International Programs Office. Student: That's good to hear. One more thing—when do participants usually hear back about acceptance? Advisor: Decisions are typically sent out by April 10th. Student: Thanks so much. I'll start working on my statement of purpose right away.

questions.stem: Why does the student visit the study abroad advisor?
options: To get information before applying to the Barcelona program
To cancel a study abroad application
To complain about a late recommendation letter
To request a refund for program fees
questions.explanation: The student wants to apply for the Barcelona program but has questions about requirements, deadlines, and logistics before submitting the application.

questions.stem: What does the advisor say about the course plan?
options: A preliminary plan is enough at the application stage
A complete course plan must be submitted by March 15th
Courses must be approved by the Barcelona university first
No course plan is required for the application
questions.explanation: The advisor says the student only needs a preliminary plan and can finalize courses after acceptance.

questions.stem: How should the recommendation letter be submitted?
options: Uploaded directly by the professor through the online portal
Mailed in a sealed envelope to the advisor's office
Attached to the student's application form
Sent via a third-party recommendation service
questions.explanation: The advisor states the professor can upload the letter directly through the online portal using her official university email.

questions.stem: What does the advisor suggest the student do about financial aid?
options: Confirm transfer details with the Financial Aid Office
Apply for a private student loan immediately
Wait until after acceptance to contact Financial Aid
Pay the full program cost upfront
questions.explanation: The advisor says most aid transfers but the student should confirm with the Financial Aid Office, and mentions possible travel grants.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the student?
options: She is well-prepared and proactive about planning
She has already been accepted into the program
She is trying to avoid the application deadline
She has never traveled outside the country
questions.explanation: The student has already secured a recommender, is asking detailed questions in advance, and plans to start her statement immediately, showing preparedness.

audioSegments.text: Hello, I'd like to apply for the study abroad program in Barcelona next semester, but I have some questions first.
Of course. The Barcelona program is very popular. What would you like to know?
I saw that the deadline is March 15th, but I haven't chosen my courses yet. Do I need a complete course plan to apply?
No, you only need a preliminary plan. You can finalize your courses after acceptance. However, you do need a letter of recommendation and a statement of purpose.
I have a professor who agreed to write a recommendation. How should she submit it?
She can upload it directly through the online portal. Make sure she uses her official university email.
Okay. Also, will my financial aid transfer to the Barcelona program?
Most federal and institutional aid does transfer, but you should confirm with the Financial Aid Office. There may also be additional travel grants available through the International Programs Office.
That's good to hear. One more thing—when do participants usually hear back about acceptance?
Decisions are typically sent out by April 10th.
Thanks so much. I'll start working on my statement of purpose right away.
